summary: This page discusses noncausal systems, using a figure to illustrate an input whose output begins before the input is applied, showing why such systems cannot be implemented in real time. It then explains why noncausal systems are still important to study, including realizable noncausal spatial systems and temporal signal-processing situations where prerecorded data makes future input values available.

figures.caption: Fig. 1.30 A noncausal system and its realization by a delayed causal system.
